title: correa glabra var. leucoclada
intro: Correa glabra var. leucoclada, also known as the white-wood correa, is a flowering plant native to Australia that belongs to the Correa genus of flowering plants in the family Rutaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, making it useful for treating various health conditions. The white-wood correa is believed to offer several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and supporting immune function, as well as having potential applications in the management of cardiovascular disease and certain types of cancer. Herbal preparations of Correa glabra var. leucoclada, such as extracts and infusions, are traditionally used in Australian traditional medicine, but more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human use.
